---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
description: "CV of Andres Martinez Gonzalez, economist at AIReF (Spanish Independent Authority for Fiscal Responsibility), Madrid. Fiscal policy, macroeconometrics and EU economic governance."
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

## Professional experience

**Economist / Analyst** — Independent Authority for Fiscal Responsibility (AIReF), Madrid  
*2021 – present*

- Quantitative analysis of fiscal policy and macroeconomic imbalances. Estimation of structural models for public debt anchor heterogeneity across countries; elasticity analysis of economic activity and the energy market.
- Analysis of EU and Spanish fiscal governance: the net expenditure path, the control account, and the reformed economic governance framework.
- Endorsement (*aval*) of the government's macroeconomic forecasts.
- Long-term growth modelling in R following the European Commission's EUCAM methodology.
- Lead of the project on external reviews of independent fiscal institutions, Economic Governance Reform Working Group, Network of EU Independent Fiscal Institutions (EUNIFI).

**Technical Advisor**, Unit for Macroeconomic Analysis — Ministry of Economy and Business, Madrid  
*2017 – 2020*

- Spain's deputy head of delegation to the Ecofin **Output Gaps Working Group**: improvement of the common methodology used to estimate potential output, output gap indicators and cyclically adjusted budget balances, a cornerstone of the surveillance of member states' fiscal policy within the Stability and Growth Pact.
- Spain's deputy head of delegation to the Ecofin **LIME Working Group**: analytical tools to assess structural reforms within the Macroeconomic Imbalances Procedure; benchmark models for imbalances related to the external sector, private sector balance sheet positions and public debt; contribution to the definition of Spain's economic policy priorities within the European Semester.
- Applied analysis on deterministic and stochastic public debt sustainability, government forecast biases, benchmarking of the Spanish housing market, the EU fiscal framework, household financial conditions, non-linear Okun's Law estimated with MARS, and exogenous and endogenous policy shocks with the in-house DSGE model EREMS.
- Participation in the elaboration of the Government's official medium-term forecasts: maintenance and development of over nineteen error correction models for real and nominal variables, used to draw the official medium-term macroeconomic outlook in the Stability Programmes, Draft Budget Laws and Draft Budgetary Plans.

**Trainee** — Caser Insurance, Madrid  
*2015*

- Market analysis and data analysis to identify profitable market segments and provide early warning of possible fraud in the vehicle and real estate branches.

## Academic experience

**Lecturer, *Advanced Analytics*** — Universidad Pontificia Comillas (ICADE), Madrid  
*2022*

- Master's level course introducing students to data analysis in R, structured in six five-hour sessions.
- Modules taught: introduction to R and RStudio, exploratory analysis of categorical and numerical variables, web scraping, and hypothesis testing.
- Practice-oriented methodology: short theoretical introduction followed by hands-on programming in RStudio, supported by technical notes, R scripts and datasets.

**Instructor** — ICEX | CECO, Madrid  
*2017 – present*

- Economic theory, statistics and the international institutional framework, taught to small groups of economics graduates. Classroom and e-learning.

## Education

**MSc in Specialized Economic Analysis: Macroeconomic Policy** — Barcelona School of Economics  
*2020 – 2021*  
Quantitative and statistical methods, fiscal policy, advanced time series and computational methods. Data science specialisation: applied machine learning, nowcasting, web scraping and unsupervised learning.

**Preparation for the High Corps of Spanish State Economics and Trade Experts**  
*2015 – 2017*  
Microeconomics, macroeconomics, international trade, international organisations, economics of the EU, public policy and the Spanish economy. Ranked 3rd among 24 successful candidates out of more than 200 initial candidates.

**Bachelor of Business** — Dundalk Institute of Technology, Ireland  
*2013 – 2014*  
Erasmus programme. International business: supply chain management, consumer psychology, statistical methods.

**Degree in Economics (Honours)** — Universidad de León  
*2011 – 2015*  
Average grade 8.4/10 over 240 ECTS. Second prize, College Economics Olympic Games, 2012. Full merit scholarship, 2011–2012. Final thesis: *Income Inequality: a Political Economy Approach*.

## Skills

**Econometrics** — time series, VAR and structural VAR, forecasting and error correction models, interrupted time series, panel data, potential output and output gap estimation.

**Programming** — R (advanced), Hansl/Gretl, Python (NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib), VBA/Excel, MATLAB, Git, Quarto.

**Languages** — Spanish (native), English (proficient), Portuguese (proficient).

## Courses and certifications

### Machine learning and statistics

- [Machine Learning with Python: from Linear Models to Deep Learning](https://courses.edx.org/certificates/8c0af874c99b40f48f445b8ecc7e12e1) (6.86x) — MITx, 2023
- [Probability: the Science of Uncertainty and Data](https://courses.edx.org/certificates/15b46d5deb2a4443b51d66640103cbf5) (6.431x) — MITx, 2020
- [Data Analysis in Social Science](https://courses.edx.org/certificates/42602f3b9ee245e28f2336224db926cb) (14.310Fx) — MITx, 2019
- Text Mining and Web Scraping — Barcelona School of Economics, 2021

### Econometrics and forecasting

- Panel Data Models — Institute for Fiscal Studies, 2018 
- Dynamic Models for Forecasting and Business Cycle Analysis — Institute for Fiscal Studies, 2018 
- National Accounts: Theory, Analysis and Applications — Institute for Fiscal Studies, 2019 
- Time Series Analysis: Forecasting Tools — Institute for Fiscal Studies, 2018 
- Introduction to Econometrics — Institute for Fiscal Studies, 2018 
- Time Series Analysis in Tramo/Seats — Ministry of Economy and Competitiveness, 2019 
- Econometrics with Gretl — Ministry of Economy and Competitiveness, 2019 

### Programming and data science

- Data Science with Python — Institute for Fiscal Studies, 2018 
- Quantitative Methods with R — Institute for Fiscal Studies, 2018 
- VBA/Excel Programming — National Statistics Institute

### Climate and energy economics

- [Macroeconomics of Climate Change: Science, Economics and Policies](https://courses.edx.org/certificates/3af7c9c486724f7a9fbd513d6435bdc8) (MCCx-SEP) — IMFx, International Monetary Fund, 2022
- [Climate Change and Sovereign Risk](https://elearning-adbi.org/certificate-verifier/?&code=63452-164-250-8684) — Asian Development Bank Institute, 2022
- The Energy Transition: Economics and Policy — Barcelona School of Economics, 2022 (11h)



## Professional activity

| | |
|---|---|
| 2017–2018 | Secretary, State Economists Association (ATCEE) |
| 2017 | Dean of Financial Affairs, Residencia César Carlos, Madrid |
| 2014–2015 | Secretary, Students' Committee, Faculty of Business, Universidad de León |
| 2011–2015 | Head of the Economics Students' Union, Universidad de León |
| 2012–2013 | Event organisation, Dundalk Institute of Technology |
| 2011–2013 | Member, Business Faculty Commission, Universidad de León |

## Contact

[andres.mtnez.glez@gmail.com](mailto:andres.mtnez.glez@gmail.com)  
[GitHub](https://github.com/AndresMtnezGlez) · [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andrés-martínez-8a034bba/) · [RPubs](https://rpubs.com/andresmtnezglez)
